chunks.text: Ancient Rome is said to have been founded in 753 BC.
For its first few centuries
it was a kingdom and then a city - state republic
only a small town on the Mediterranean.
By the second century BC, Rome had united the Italian peninsula and
after a long war with Carthage, taken hold of the power of the Mediterranean.
The senate and the consul of the republic governed this vast territory.
In the first century BC, Julius Caesar appeared.
He conquered Gaul and brought to Rome vast wealth and territory.
Eventually he became dictator for life, but in 44 BC he was assassinated by senators.
His successor Octavian, in 27 BC
was given the title " Augustus " by the senate and became the first emperor.
Through this, Rome was transformed from a republic into an empire.
The two centuries that followed are called the Pax Romana, a period of prosperity.
The whole shore of the Mediterranean fell under Roman rule, and roads were built
with aqueducts, the Colosseum, and baths constructed across the lands.
But from the third century onward
the Roman Empire would suffer from internal disorder and the invasion of outsiders.
In 395, the empire split into east and west, and in 476
the western empire was brought down by Germanic peoples.
The story of an empire that had lasted for over a thousand years was thus closed.
Rome vanished, but its legacy still lives strongly in the law, language, architecture
and political system of Europe today.
